LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PATRICK DOHERTY, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION AND DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION + DOYLE DEVELOPMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE PLAINTIFF IS ATTEMPTING TO DO WHAT IS EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS ARGUMENT THAT THERE IS A BASIS FOR RECISSION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT CONVINCING. INFORMATION REGARDING LICENSING OF DEFENDANTS IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC RECORD WHICH WAS INFORMATION EASILY OBTAINABLE BY PLANTIFFS AT THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO. CARROLL V. DUNGEY (1966) 223 CAL.APP.2D 247, 255. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT REFERENCED IN THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. BECAUSE THE PLEADINGS MUST DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF RELEVANT ISSUES ON A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT CANNOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL'S PLEA OF "OVERSIGHT" OR "MISTAKE". NIETO V. BLUE SHIELD OF CAL. LIFE & HEALTH INS. CO. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 73. PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT DOYLE IS DENIED. FINALLY PLAINTIFFS OBJECTIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED AS THEY WERE NOT PROPERLY FILED WITH THE OPPOSITION BRIEF AS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1354(A). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KATY BERSAMIN, CSR #11722

Notice Of Intent Ot Move For New To Vacate Reconsider Or Set Aside Order Or Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs Jane Melc, George Melc's Motion to Vacate Order of Dismissal is DENIED. ALTHOUGH ALL THE PARTIES IN THE COMPLAINT HAD BEEN DISPOSED OF EITHER BY DISMISSAL OR JUDGMENT, THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AND DOES 1-100 OF COMPLAINTI WERE NEVER DISMISSED. AS THERE WERE NO APPEARANCES AT THE MARCH 1, 2012 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: DISMISSAL PURSUANT TO CRC 3.1385, THE COURT THERBY DISMISSED THE BALANCE OF THE CASE. SHOULD PLAINTIFFS PREVAIL ON THEIR APPEAL OF THE GRANTING OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, JURISDICTION OVER THE COMPLAINT WILL REVERT BACK TO THIS COURT WITH THE FILING OF THE REMITTITUR. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFFS WERE NOT PREJUDICED BY THE COURT'S DISMISSAL. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion For An Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS PATRICK DOHERTY, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION & DOYLE DEVELOPMENT'S MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS.
Appearances: Paul F. Utrecht of Zacks & Utrecht (956.8100) for defendants and moving parties; George W. Wolff of the Law Office George William Wolff (788.1881) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted without any multiplier. Moving parties are entitled to the fees sought and the lodestar is reasonable. Order, and Amended Judgment signed in open court. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-08-2011 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2011 Off Calendar

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-27-2011 Continued To Nov-09-11 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS PATRICK DOHERTY, DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION AND DOHERTY CONSTRUCTION + DOYLE DEVELOPMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: George W. Wolff, Esq. of Law Office of George William Wolff 788-1881 for pltfs & opposing parties; Scott A. Freedman, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht 956-8100 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION, THE PLAINTIFF IS ATTEMPTING TO DO WHAT IS EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED UNDER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS ARGUMENT THAT THERE IS A BASIS FOR RECISSION OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS NOT CONVINCING. INFORMATION REGARDING LICENSING OF DEFENDANTS IS A MATTER OF PUBLIC RECORD WHICH WAS INFORMATION EASILY OBTAINABLE BY PLANTIFFS AT THE TIME THE SETTLEMENT WAS ENTERED INTO. CARROLL V. DUNGEY (1966) 223 CAL.APP.2D 247, 255.
AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PARTIES TO THE CONTRACT REFERENCED IN THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. BECAUSE THE PLEADINGS MUST DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF RELEVANT ISSUES ON A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE COURT CANNOT CONSIDER PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL'S PLEA OF "OVERSIGHT" OR "MISTAKE". NIETO V. BLUE SHIELD OF CAL. LIFE & HEALTH INS. CO. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 73.
PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE TO CONDUCT THE DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT DOYLE IS DENIED.
FINALLY PLAINTIFFS OBJECTIONS WILL NOT BE CONSIDERED AS THEY WERE NOT PROPERLY FILED WITH THE OPPOSITION BRIEF AS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1354(a). Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722
